title: AGARICUS BLAZEI
description: Agaricus blazei Murill (ABM), also known as the Brazilian mushroom or jisongrong, is a medicinal mushroom containing bioactive compounds including β-glucans, polysaccharides, polypeptides, and ergosterol. It works primarily through immunomodulation, antioxidant activity, and modulation of the Th1/Th2 immune balance, with demonstrated effects on inflammation, oxidative stress, and metabolic pathways including Keap1/Nrf2/ARE signaling.

research.title: The mushroom Agaricus Blazei Murill in combination with metformin and gliclazide improves insulin resistance in type 2 diabetes: a randomized, double-blinded, and placebo-controlled clinical trial
research.summary: In a randomized controlled trial of 72 type 2 diabetes patients, ABM extract (1500 mg daily for 12 weeks) combined with standard diabetes medications significantly improved insulin resistance measured by HOMA-IR.

research.title: Dietary supplementation with Agaricus blazei murill extract prevents diet-induced obesity and insulin resistance in rats
research.summary: ABM supplementation protected rats against high-fat diet-induced obesity, insulin resistance, glucose intolerance, and inflammation markers without reducing food intake, suggesting metabolic benefits independent of caloric restriction.

research.title: Agaricus blazei polypeptide exerts a protective effect on D-galactose-induced aging mice via the Keap1/Nrf2/ARE and P53/Trim32 signaling pathways
research.summary: A novel 3 kD polypeptide from ABM significantly improved antioxidant markers (T-AOC, CAT) and reduced oxidative stress (MDA, ROS) in aging mice through activation of key longevity-related signaling pathways.

research.title: Phase I Clinical Study of the Dietary Supplement, Agaricus blazei Murill, in Cancer Patients in Remission
research.summary: Safety study of 78 cancer survivors taking 1.8-5.4g ABM daily for 6 months found only 12% experienced adverse events (mostly mild digestive issues), with no dose-dependent toxicity, supporting safety at tested dosages.

research.title: Hot water extract of Agaricus blazei Murrill specifically inhibits growth and induces apoptosis in human pancreatic cancer cells
research.summary: ABM hot water extract demonstrated specific anti-cancer activity against human pancreatic cancer cell lines through induction of apoptosis, suggesting potential therapeutic applications for pancreatic cancer.

research.title: The beneficial effects of Agaricus blazei Murrill on hepatic antioxidant enzymes and the pancreatic tissue recovery in streptozotocin-induced diabetic rats
research.summary: ABM extracts, particularly ethyl acetate extract, improved hepatic antioxidant enzyme activity (SOD, CAT, glutathione) and promoted pancreatic tissue recovery in diabetic rats, suggesting hepatoprotective and pancreatic regenerative effects.

research.title: Gastroprotective effect of hydroalcoholic extract from Agaricus blazei Murill against ethanol-induced gastric ulcer in mice
research.summary: ABM extract demonstrated protective effects against ethanol-induced gastric ulcers in mice, showing gastroprotective properties through anti-inflammatory and antioxidant mechanisms.

research.title: Hypolipidemic effect of the edible mushroom Agaricus blazei in rats subjected to a hypercholesterolemic diet
research.summary: 1% ABM supplementation for 6 weeks significantly reduced total cholesterol, non-HDL cholesterol, and atherogenic index while decreasing abdominal fat weight in rats fed a high cholesterol diet.

research.title: Pro-inflammatory effects of the mushroom Agaricus blazei and its consequences on atherosclerosis development
research.summary: In ApoE-deficient mice, 6-12 weeks of ABM supplementation unexpectedly enhanced leukocyte activation and migration, potentially increasing atherosclerosis development, raising safety concerns for cardiovascular-compromised individuals.

sentiment.summary: User experiences show mixed results, with some incorporating ABM into comprehensive stacks for immune and metabolic support, while others question the value relative to cost and express concerns about over-supplementation. Limited standalone discussion suggests ABM is typically used as part of broader supplement regimens rather than as a primary intervention.

effects.label: Limited Standalone Data:
effects.description: Most Reddit users take ABM as part of complex multi-supplement stacks, making isolated effects difficult to assess

effects.label: Supporting Role:
effects.description: Typically used as a complementary ingredient rather than primary therapeutic agent in user protocols

effects.label: Research-Practice Gap:
effects.description: Strong research support for metabolic and immune benefits, but limited user discussion of specific effects

effects.label: Professional Guidance:
effects.description: Users seeking significant health improvements are frequently advised to consult healthcare providers rather than rely on supplement stacks alone

dosage.label: Clinical Range:
dosage.description: Research studies used 1,500-5,400 mg daily of ABM extract, typically divided into 2-3 doses

dosage.label: Mushroom Blends:
dosage.description: Users commonly take 143-1,000 mg of ABM mycelium as part of multi-mushroom formulations

dosage.label: Duration:
dosage.description: Clinical safety established for 6-12 weeks; some users report longer-term use as part of maintenance stacks

dosage.label: Fermented Forms:
dosage.description: Some brands offer fermented ABM claiming improved bioavailability, though user verification is limited

sideEffects.label: Digestive Issues:
sideEffects.description: Clinical trials reported mild nausea and diarrhea in about 12% of users, typically non-serious

sideEffects.label: Allergic Reactions:
sideEffects.description: One documented case of liver dysfunction related to food allergy/drug lymphocyte product in clinical study

sideEffects.label: Cardiovascular Concerns:
sideEffects.description: Research in ApoE-deficient mice showed pro-inflammatory effects and potential atherosclerosis promotion, suggesting caution for those with cardiovascular issues

sideEffects.label: Generally Well-Tolerated:
sideEffects.description: Most users and studies report good tolerability with minimal adverse effects at recommended doses

availability.label: Multiple Brands:
availability.description: Available from companies like quality brands, SoTru, and various medicinal mushroom specialists, with varying forms (powder, capsules, fermented)

availability.label: Quality Concerns:
availability.description: Users express difficulty verifying quality and active compound content, with brands often unable to provide comprehensive COAs or research backing fermentation claims

availability.label: Pricing:
availability.description: Considered expensive by users, particularly in proprietary blends, with bulk powder forms potentially more cost-effective
